ai_insights.executive_summary: CCI operated across 9 locations in February 2026. Scheduler compliance ranged from 71.5% (CCI) to 92.9% (14th Ave).
highlights: Florence delivered exceptional gains, with scheduler compliance rising from 55.8% in January to 88.1% in February, a 57.8% improvement that positions the clinic well above the company average of 78.27%, and average delay falling from 3.8 minutes in January to 2.2 minutes in February, a 42.2% reduction. 14th Ave similarly strengthened performance, with scheduler compliance climbing from 66.5% in January to 92.9% in February (a 39.6% gain and the highest in the network), while treatments running past closing time declined from 0.7 per day in January to 0.5 per day in February, demonstrating improved close-time discipline.
concerns: We noticed that CCI-E Pod is experiencing average patient delays of 15.6 minutes, 147% above the company average of 6.31 minutes—the largest performance gap across the network—despite maintaining high chair utilization of 90.4%; reviewing scheduling templates and appointment spacing logic within iOptimize for this pod may reveal capacity-demand misalignment. Additionally, CCI-CD Pod is running 3.3 treatments past closing per day, 2.1 standard deviations above the group mean of 1.0, suggesting either scheduling conflicts late in the day or workflow bottlenecks; pairing this with an audit of nurse-to-chair assignments during peak afternoon hours could identify where bottlenecks occur and inform staffing adjustments.
recommendations: We recommend that CCI-E Pod pilot a focused review of its scheduling template adherence and iOptimize assignment logic, working with the scheduling team to test whether tighter appointment spacing or nurse rebalancing alleviates the 15.6-minute average delay. This targeted diagnostic, rather than a broad operational audit, will help us understand whether the issue stems from scheduling discipline, nurse capacity, or patient-flow friction—and can serve as a model for the other three CCI pods currently performing near network average.

scheduler_compliance.label: Scheduler Compliance
scheduler_compliance.explanation: Percentage of appointments scheduled following iOptimize recommendations. Higher is better. Company average is typically 60-70%. OncoSmart benchmark target is 75%.

avg_delay_mins.label: Avg Delay
avg_delay_mins.explanation: Average daily schedule delay in minutes. Lower is better. Measures how far behind the clinic is running on average.

avg_treatments_per_day.label: Tx Past Close/Day
avg_treatments_per_day.explanation: Average treatments running past treatment close per day (overtime patients per day). Lower is better.

avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.label: Tx Mins Past Close/Patient
avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.explanation: Average treatment minutes past closing time per patient. Lower is better.

avg_chair_utilization.label: Chair Utilization
avg_chair_utilization.explanation: Average chair utilization rate. Higher is better.

iassign_utilization.label: iAssign Utilization
iassign_utilization.explanation: Percentage of nurse assignments completed via iAssign. Higher means more consistent, optimized assignments.

avg_patients_per_nurse.label: Patients/Nurse/Day
avg_patients_per_nurse.explanation: Average patients per nurse per day. Context-dependent -- too high means understaffing, too low means inefficiency.

avg_chairs_per_nurse.label: Chairs/Nurse
avg_chairs_per_nurse.explanation: Average chairs assigned per nurse. Context-dependent.

avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.label: Nurse Utilization
avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.explanation: Average nurse-to-patient in-chair time per day. Higher is better.

chatbot_context.data_notes: Company Avg rows represent the network average across all locations for this client in the given month. Onco rows are global oncology benchmarks from the OncoSmart network. Rows named Global Avg, Network Avg, Total, or Overall are network-wide aggregates, not individual clinic locations. MoM (Month-over-Month) deltas compare the current month to the prior month. Composite scores are weighted 0 to 100 where 100 is best-in-class performance.
